claims.claim: High animal protein intake increases risk of type 2 diabetes by 20-40% for every 10g consumed above 64g/day
claims.mechanism: Activates insulin/IGF-1/mTOR pathway promoting insulin resistance; branched-chain amino acids (leucine, isoleucine, valine) and sulphur amino acids (methionine) directly stimulate mTOR signalling

claims.claim: High animal protein intake associated with 75% increase in overall mortality and 4-fold increase in cancer and diabetes mortality in ages 50-65
claims.mechanism: Activation of IGF-1/mTOR pro-ageing and pro-cancer pathway; excessive methionine and branched-chain amino acids accelerate ageing
claims.study_ref: Ref 43 in book (NHANES study of 6381 adults, 18-year follow-up)

claims.claim: Animal protein consumption drives hypercholesterolemia via saturated fat content
claims.mechanism: Saturated fat in meat and dairy raises blood LDL-cholesterol; for every 1% increase in calories from saturated fat, LDL rises by 2%
claims.study_ref: Refs 46, 64 in book (including Seven Countries study)

claims.claim: Choline and L-carnitine in animal products accelerate coronary atherosclerosis
claims.mechanism: Gut bacteria ferment choline and L-carnitine into TMAO (trimethylamine N-oxide), which accelerates formation of coronary atherosclerotic plaques

claims.claim: Excess iron from animal products drives oxidation and colon cancer
claims.mechanism: Heme iron promotes oxidative stress and is specifically linked to colon cancer development

claims.claim: High protein intake prevents the expected improvement in insulin sensitivity from weight loss
claims.mechanism: 1.3g protein/kg body weight blocked insulin sensitivity improvement despite 10% weight loss and visceral fat reduction in obese women; insulin resistance maintained via insulin/IGF pathway activation
claims.study_ref: Ref 42 in book (Bettina Mittendorfer, Washington University clinical trial)

claims.claim: High-protein diets from dairy/meat cause drastic reduction in insulin sensitivity
claims.mechanism: Dairy, meat products and whey protein supplements activate pathways that reduce insulin sensitivity and increase blood insulin levels

consumption.amount: Limit to 0.8g protein/kg body weight/day total (about 58g for a 70kg person); no more than 30g protein per meal
consumption.frequency: Minimise animal sources; favour plant protein
consumption.reasoning: Protein synthesis plateaus at ~30g per meal (Refs 27,28); excess activates pro-ageing IGF/mTOR pathway (Ref 29); Okinawan centenarians consumed only 9% protein diet

relationships.description: Substituting plant protein for animal protein lowers mortality 12-34% depending on the animal protein replaced (Ref 60)
Optimal longevity diet has protein-to-carb ratio of ~1:10 with protein under 10% of calories (Ref 44)

claims.claim: Substituting 3% of energy from processed red meat with plant protein reduces all-cause mortality by 34%
claims.mechanism: Eliminates TMAO production from choline/L-carnitine, reduces heme iron-driven oxidation, lowers methionine and BCAA intake that drive IGF/mTOR pathway
claims.study_ref: Ref 60 in book (Harvard Physician and Nurse Health Studies)

consumption.reasoning: Highest mortality association of all animal protein sources

relationships.description: Plant protein substitution provides the largest mortality reduction (34%) when replacing processed red meat

claims.claim: Substituting 3% of energy from unprocessed red meat with plant protein reduces all-cause mortality by 12%
claims.mechanism: Same mechanisms as processed red meat but somewhat less harmful; still drives TMAO, heme iron oxidation and mTOR activation
claims.study_ref: Ref 60 in book (Harvard Physician and Nurse Health Studies)

consumption.reasoning: 12% mortality reduction when replaced with plant protein

claims.claim: Substituting plant protein for egg protein reduces all-cause mortality by 19%
claims.mechanism: Eggs contain choline (converted to TMAO by gut bacteria), branched-chain amino acids that activate IGF/mTOR pathway, and cholesterol
claims.study_ref: Ref 60 in book (Harvard Physician and Nurse Health Studies)

claims.claim: High consumption of eggs prevents expected glucose metabolism improvements during weight loss
claims.mechanism: Part of animal protein group (chicken, fish, eggs, low-fat milk, cheeses) that blocked insulin sensitivity improvements in type 2 diabetes patients during 2-month weight loss

consumption.amount: Limit; lacto-ovo vegetarians can use for B12
consumption.reasoning: 19% mortality reduction when replaced with plant protein

relationships.description: Eggs are a source of B12 for lacto-ovo vegetarians

claims.claim: No association or inverse association between plant protein consumption and type 2 diabetes
claims.mechanism: Lower in branched-chain amino acids and methionine than animal protein; does not activate IGF/mTOR pathway as strongly; associated with beneficial bioactive substances

claims.claim: Substituting plant for animal protein markedly inhibits prostate and breast cancer growth
claims.mechanism: Lower branched-chain amino acids reduce mTOR-driven cancer cell proliferation; reduced methionine limits cancer cell metabolism
claims.study_ref: Ref 58 in book (human xenograft animal models)

claims.claim: Reduced branched-chain amino acid diet (characteristic of plant protein) reduces body weight, fat and blood glucose
claims.mechanism: Specifically reducing leucine, isoleucine and valine intake is sufficient to lower body weight and blood glucose in mice
claims.study_ref: Ref 55 in book (author's study with mice)

claims.claim: Plant protein associated with lower all-cause mortality vs animal protein
claims.mechanism: Lower methionine content; methionine restriction extends average and maximal lifespan by up to 30% in rodents and reduces glucose, IGF-1 and insulin levels

claims.claim: Moderate protein reduction (7-9% of calories from protein) causes weight loss and doubles FGF21 longevity hormone
claims.mechanism: Low protein intake triggers FGF21 (fibroblast growth factor 21) production, a pro-longevity hormone; subjects lost 2.6kg in 4 weeks and had significantly lower fasting blood glucose
claims.study_ref: Refs 39, 40 in book (author's clinical trial + Dudley Lamming collaboration)

consumption.amount: Should comprise majority of protein intake; aim for ~0.8g total protein/kg body weight/day with most from plants
consumption.preparation: Combine legumes with whole grains during the day to get complete amino acid profile
consumption.reasoning: Legumes high in lysine but low in tryptophan/methionine; grains are the reverse; combined they provide complete protein equivalent to eggs or meat

relationships.description: Brown rice + lentils provides complete protein with all essential amino acids
Durum wheat pasta + chickpeas provides complete protein

ingredients.name: Legumes (lentils, chickpeas, fava beans, black beans)

claims.claim: Provide high-lysine plant protein that complements grain protein for complete amino acid profile
claims.mechanism: High in lysine, low in tryptophan and methionine; when combined with grains (high tryptophan/methionine, low lysine) provides complete protein identical to animal sources

claims.claim: 100g cooked lentils provides 8g fibre vs 0g in 100g beef
claims.mechanism: Fibre feeds beneficial gut bacteria producing short-chain fatty acids; dilutes carcinogens in colon; slows glucose absorption

claims.claim: Sprouting or fermenting beans destroys phytates, increasing mineral absorption
claims.mechanism: Phytates in raw legumes chelate iron, zinc and calcium, inhibiting absorption; sprouting/fermentation enzymatically degrades phytates

consumption.amount: Regular inclusion; e.g. 1 cup lentils provides substantial fibre
consumption.preparation: Cooked; sprouting or fermenting recommended to reduce phytates and increase mineral absorption
consumption.reasoning: Core protein and fibre source; combine with whole grains for complete amino acids

relationships.description: Complementary amino acid profiles create complete protein when combined
Vitamin C from lemon juice increases iron and mineral absorption from legumes

claims.claim: Contains 3x more fibre than white rice (3g vs 1g per cup)
claims.mechanism: Fibre-rich cell wall restricts rate of starch digestion and absorption; slow glucose release reduces insulin spikes

claims.claim: Part of a sample daily diet providing 73g fibre in only 1886 kcal with complete nutrition
claims.mechanism: 2 cups brown rice + 1 cup lentils + 20 almonds + vegetables + fruit + olive oil + flax seeds = 73g fibre, 76g protein, 64g fat (4% saturated), >100% RDI all nutrients except D and B12

consumption.preparation: Cooked but not overcooked; overcooking gelatinises starch causing rapid glucose absorption
consumption.reasoning: Overcooked rice disrupts starch granules making them easily available to pancreatic amylases

relationships.description: Brown rice + lentils = complete protein with all essential amino acids
Brown rice should replace white rice which has high glycaemic index and load

claims.claim: Monounsaturated fat from plant origins associated with lower cardiovascular and cancer death rate
claims.mechanism: Contains primarily monounsaturated fatty acids that do not increase cholesterol; reduces LDL without affecting HDL; reduces blood sugar and triglycerides in diabetes
claims.study_ref: Refs 66, 67, 68, 69 in book (Nurses' Health Study and others)

claims.claim: Slows absorption of carbohydrates when added to meals
claims.mechanism: Fat delays gastric emptying and intestinal carbohydrate absorption, lowering glycaemic spike

claims.claim: Excellent source of vitamin E and precious phytochemicals
claims.mechanism: Cold-pressed extraction preserves vitamin E (antioxidant) and polyphenols with anti-inflammatory properties

claims.claim: Plant-origin monounsaturated fats reduce coronary heart disease risk
claims.mechanism: Substituting monounsaturated fat for saturated fat reduces LDL-cholesterol without reducing HDL-cholesterol

consumption.amount: 2 tablespoons per day (in sample diet); use in moderation
consumption.preparation: Cold-pressed extra-virgin; used as main condiment; avoid heating to high temperatures
consumption.reasoning: Author recommends as primary fat source replacing butter, margarine and vegetable oils; must be cold-pressed to preserve phytochemicals

relationships.description: Adding olive oil to carbohydrate meals slows glucose absorption
Olive oil recommended over omega-6-rich vegetable oils which promote inflammation
Olive oil should replace butter (saturated fat) and margarine (trans fats)

claims.claim: Plant-origin monounsaturated fat associated with lower cardiovascular and cancer death rate
claims.mechanism: Contains monounsaturated fatty acids that do not increase cholesterol and protect the heart; plant-origin MUFA specifically associated with lower mortality vs animal-origin MUFA

consumption.reasoning: Listed alongside olive oil and nuts as healthy monounsaturated fat source

relationships.description: Both provide plant-origin monounsaturated fats

claims.claim: Contain monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fatty acids that protect the heart and do not increase cholesterol
claims.mechanism: Monounsaturated fats reduce LDL without affecting HDL; polyunsaturated fats provide essential fatty acids

claims.claim: Part of healthy plant-based diet associated with 25% lower risk of coronary heart disease
claims.mechanism: Component of healthier plant food pattern that collectively reduces cardiovascular risk

consumption.reasoning: Included in author's sample complete daily diet providing 73g fibre

relationships.description: Both provide healthy plant-origin fats

claims.claim: Part of a nutritionally complete daily diet
claims.mechanism: Provides protein, healthy fats, fibre and minerals as part of a plant-based diet

consumption.reasoning: Specific quantity cited in author's sample daily diet

claims.claim: Part of a nutritionally complete daily diet providing >100% RDI of most nutrients
claims.mechanism: Provides omega-3 fatty acids (ALA), fibre and lignans

consumption.reasoning: Specific quantity cited in author's sample daily diet

claims.claim: Al dente spaghetti produces much lower glycaemic spike than white rice
claims.mechanism: Dense food matrix of durum wheat flour is digested more slowly than disrupted starch granules in overcooked rice; restricts rate of starch access by pancreatic amylases

consumption.preparation: Cooked al dente (not overcooked); combined with chickpeas for complete protein
consumption.reasoning: Dense food matrix of durum wheat makes it a slow-releasing carbohydrate

Adding olive oil further slows carbohydrate absorption

ingredients.name: Whole Grains (barley, farro, oats, brown rice)

claims.claim: Regular consumption reduces cardiovascular disease risk by 20-40% and diabetes risk by 20-30%
claims.mechanism: Fibre-rich cell wall restricts rate of starch/sugar digestion and absorption; slow glucose release reduces insulin spikes; low glycaemic index induces satiety

claims.claim: Highest intake of wholegrain fibre associated with 19% lower all-cause mortality and 15-34% lower disease-specific mortality
claims.mechanism: Fibre increases stool bulk (diluting carcinogens), increases bile acid excretion, alters colonic flora, and generates short-chain fatty acids that nourish colon cells and immune cells
claims.study_ref: Ref 90 in book (NIH-AARP Diet and Health Study, 367,442 participants, 14-year follow-up)

claims.claim: Part of healthy plant-based diet pattern with 25% lower coronary heart disease risk
claims.mechanism: Complex carbohydrate source that avoids insulin spikes while providing fibre, vitamins and minerals

consumption.amount: Sufficient to achieve 45g+ fibre per day (traditional Mediterranean level)
consumption.preparation: Minimally processed; not overcooked; sourdough fermentation for bread to reduce phytates
consumption.reasoning: Traditional Mediterranean diet provided 45g/day fibre (3x Western average of 14g/day)

relationships.description: Complementary amino acid profiles; combined provide complete protein
Olive oil slows carbohydrate absorption from grains
Whole grains must replace refined grains (white bread, white rice) which behave like simple sugar

claims.claim: Consuming 14g/day fibre vs 30g+ increases mortality risk by 30%
claims.mechanism: Multiple protective mechanisms: increases stool bulk diluting carcinogens, faster colon transit, reduces gut pH, increases bile acid excretion, alters colonic flora, generates short-chain fatty acids for colon cells
claims.study_ref: Ref 85 in book (large European epidemiological study)

claims.claim: Each 10g/day increase in fibre reduces mortality risk by 10%
claims.mechanism: Dose-dependent protection via multiple gut and systemic mechanisms

claims.claim: 35g fibre/day reduces colon cancer risk by 40% vs 15g/day
claims.mechanism: Larger stools dilute carcinogens and speed transit; fibre reduces pH; short-chain fatty acid production nourishes colon mucosa and immune cells

claims.claim: 25-29g/day fibre reduces risk of death from diabetes, heart attack, colorectal and breast cancer
claims.mechanism: Fibre slows glucose absorption (reduces diabetes risk), feeds beneficial gut bacteria, produces anti-inflammatory short-chain fatty acids

claims.claim: Higher fibre intake significantly lowers body weight, systolic blood pressure and total cholesterol
claims.mechanism: Fibre induces satiety via slower gastric emptying; reduces calorie absorption; binds bile acids reducing cholesterol

claims.claim: Insoluble fibre generates short-chain fatty acids that are preferred fuel for colon mucosa and local immune cells
claims.mechanism: Gut bacteria ferment insoluble fibre into short-chain fatty acids (butyrate, propionate, acetate) which nourish colonocytes and regulate immune function

consumption.amount: Minimum 25-29g/day; ideally 45g+ (Mediterranean level); sample diet achieves 73g in 1886 kcal
consumption.preparation: From whole foods only; NOT from fibre supplements (supplements may increase tumour recurrence risk per Ref 96)
consumption.reasoning: Fibre supplements (ispaghula husk) showed increased tumour recurrence in 3-year RCT (Ref 96); whole food matrix is essential for benefits

relationships.description: Fibre supplements do not replicate whole food benefits and may increase tumour recurrence (Ref 96, 3-year RCT with ispaghula husk)

claims.claim: 3.5g/day fibre supplements significantly increase risk of small and large tumour recurrence
claims.mechanism: Complex interactions among nutritional factors; supplements lack the structural food matrix properties of whole foods; risk especially elevated with high calcium intake
claims.study_ref: Ref 96 in book (3-year randomised clinical trial)

consumption.reasoning: Clinical trial evidence of harm; whole food fibre is the only safe and effective approach

relationships.description: High calcium intake combined with fibre supplements especially increases tumour recurrence risk

claims.claim: High intake promotes inflammation, coronary thrombosis and cancer in animal studies
claims.mechanism: Omega-6 polyunsaturated fatty acids are precursors to pro-inflammatory eicosanoids; promote inflammatory cascades

claims.claim: Quickly become rancid at room temperature and often extracted with chemical solvents
claims.mechanism: Polyunsaturated bonds are chemically unstable and oxidise readily; solvent extraction introduces chemical residues

consumption.preparation: If used, keep refrigerated; prefer cold-pressed
consumption.reasoning: Animal studies show harm; not yet confirmed in humans but author recommends limiting in favour of olive oil

relationships.description: Should be replaced with cold-pressed extra-virgin olive oil

claims.claim: Worse than butter and cream in increasing blood cholesterol, inflammation and heart attack risk
claims.mechanism: Trans fatty acids increase LDL-cholesterol, lower HDL-cholesterol, promote inflammation and accelerate atherosclerotic plaque formation

consumption.reasoning: Worse than even saturated animal fat for cardiovascular risk

relationships.description: Olive oil should replace margarine as primary fat

ingredients.name: Refined Grains (white bread, white rice, cornflakes)

claims.claim: Behave like simple sugar causing steep blood glucose spikes and insulin surges
claims.mechanism: Milling removes fibre-rich cell wall that restricts starch digestion rate; stripped starch is rapidly digested by pancreatic amylases causing glucose spikes that trigger insulin overshoot, subsequent hypoglycaemia, hunger and fat deposition

claims.claim: Adding fibre supplements to refined grains does not restore lost structural food matrix properties
claims.mechanism: The physical food matrix structure found in whole grains cannot be replicated by adding isolated fibre back

consumption.reasoning: Possess both very high glycaemic index and very high glucose load

relationships.description: Must be replaced with whole grain equivalents

claims.claim: Very high glycaemic index AND very high glucose load - should be eliminated or drastically reduced
claims.mechanism: Starchy vegetables with minimal fibre resistance to digestion; rapid glucose absorption triggers insulin spikes
claims.study_ref: book-assertion based on glycaemic index data

consumption.reasoning: Listed alongside white bread and white rice as foods with both high GI and high glucose load

claims.claim: Low glycaemic impact among fruits
claims.mechanism: Smaller effect on blood glucose due to lower sugar content and higher fibre relative to other fruits

consumption.reasoning: Listed among lower glycaemic index fruits alongside apples and prunes

claims.claim: Lower glycaemic impact; should be eaten whole not juiced
claims.mechanism: Whole fruit food matrix and fibre slow sugar digestion; juicing destroys matrix and releases free sugars for rapid absorption

consumption.reasoning: Juicing removes structural food matrix properties

ingredients.name: Green Vegetables (lettuce, cabbage, broccoli, spinach, arugula, chard, kale)

claims.mechanism: Very low carbohydrate content and high fibre means virtually no glucose load

consumption.reasoning: Superior calcium source to dairy for bioavailability; important for vegans

Vitamin D facilitates calcium absorption

claims.claim: High oxalate content hinders calcium absorption

consumption.amount: Can eat for other nutrients but not as calcium source
consumption.reasoning: Still nutritious but unreliable calcium sources due to oxalate content

relationships.description: Complement with low-oxalate vegetables for calcium needs

claims.claim: Despite high glycaemic index, very low carbohydrate load makes them healthy
claims.mechanism: High GI but very low total carbohydrate content means negligible actual glucose load; provides vitamins and phytochemicals
claims.study_ref: book-assertion (glycaemic index vs glucose load distinction)

consumption.preparation: Whole, not juiced (juicing destroys food matrix per Ref 76)
consumption.reasoning: Should not be eliminated despite high GI because glucose load is low and nutritional value is high

claims.claim: Increases iron and zinc absorption from plant foods

consumption.amount: Juice of 1 lemon per day (in sample diet)
consumption.frequency: With meals containing legumes, grains and vegetables
consumption.preparation: Fresh squeezed; used to cook and season vegetables, legumes and grains

relationships.description: Vitamin C dramatically increases iron absorption from legumes
Increases mineral absorption from grains
Tea, coffee and cocoa reduce mineral absorption during meals - opposite effect to vitamin C

consumption.frequency: Consume between meals, not with food

claims.mechanism: B12 is produced by soil bacteria and incorporated into animal products; humans cannot produce it; essential for proper cell function; chronic deficiency linked to cognitive decline

claims.mechanism: Folic acid corrects the megaloblastic anaemia of B12 deficiency but does not prevent neurological damage, allowing silent progression

consumption.reasoning: Vegans have no dietary source; must supplement or consume fortified foods
